what_it_does: Defines a React inbox canvas where users can drop or browse for files, have uploaded items classified into design-system categories through window.claude, then apply, retag, discard, bulk-select, or clear tagged items.
observations: The file defines a fixed CATEGORIES list for icons, colors, logos, imagery, voice, components, templates, and type.
Image uploads are read as data URLs and sent to window.claude with image content plus filename context.
Non-image uploads are classified from filename, MIME type, and formatted size.
Uploaded items remain pending until a suggested or manually chosen category is applied.
The UI includes drag-and-drop state, thumbnail previews, per-item actions, category picker popover, and bulk-selection controls.
Classification failures fall back to the imagery category.
The component exports itself by assigning Inbox to window.Inbox.
standouts: The inbox treats AI tagging as a proposal that must be explicitly accepted before an item is marked applied.
The visual classification path sends actual image data rather than relying only on file metadata.
The component combines intake, classification, manual override, and bulk operations in one file.
conventions: Uses React function components and hook aliases from the global React object.
Uses inline style objects for several layout and state-specific UI details.
Uses global app primitives such as CanvasHeader, ViShape, CvIcon, window.claude, and window.dsaToast.
Uses Set state for selected item IDs and immutable item-list updates through setItems.
Exposes the component through a global window assignment instead of an ES module export.
concerns: autoTag reads the current item through a setItems updater side effect, which couples lookup to React state scheduling.
handleFiles queues item insertion and immediately calls autoTag, so classification depends on queued state updates being observed in order.
bulkApply reports the count of selected IDs, even when some selected items may not have a suggested category to apply.
applyAllSuggestions performs one setItems call per eligible item rather than a single batched list transformation.
The category picker is rendered inline with relative positioning, which may be sensitive to surrounding layout constraints.
notes: This file is the intake and triage surface for bringing uploaded design assets into category buckets under user approval.
questions: What shape does window.claude.complete return in this runtime?
Are applied inbox items meant to stay visible until cleared, or should applying immediately remove them from the inbox?
Should selected applied items remain selectable or be removed from selection when an item becomes applied?
purpose_vs_actual: The header comment says this is a drop zone with real AI tagging, and the file implements a drag-and-drop file inbox with Claude-backed category suggestions and approval controls.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to reduce manual sorting work for design-system materials while keeping the final filing decision under user control.
novelty: It embeds visual AI classification directly into a design-system inbox workflow and presents AI output as a clickable, overridable tag proposal.
contribution: This file contributes a user-mediated asset intake pattern: dropped files become reviewable inbox items with metadata, previews, AI category suggestions, manual category overrides, and bulk filing actions.
summary_for_embedding: A React inbox canvas for uploading design-system assets, previewing them, classifying them with Claude into fixed categories, and applying or rejecting suggested tags individually or in bulk.
intention_for_embedding: This was for turning unsorted brand and design files into reviewed, categorized design-system inputs with AI assistance and explicit user approval.

symbols.does: Reads a File object as a data URL through FileReader.
symbols.description: This async helper wraps FileReader in a Promise and resolves with the loaded data URL. It is used for image uploads so the image can be previewed and passed to the visual classification request.

symbols.does: Formats a byte count as B, KB, or MB.
symbols.description: This helper converts raw file sizes into short display strings. It also supplies readable size text for the non-image classification prompt.

symbols.does: Renders the inbox canvas and owns upload, tagging, selection, and bulk workflow state.
symbols.description: The component presents the drop zone, empty state, pending item list, bulk toolbar, and section controls. It coordinates file ingestion, AI tagging, manual application of categories, rejection, retagging, and clearing applied items through the supplied items and setItems props.

symbols.does: Toggles one inbox item ID in the selected Set.
symbols.description: This function clones the current selected ID set, adds the item ID when absent, removes it when present, and stores the new Set. It supports per-row checkbox selection for bulk actions.

symbols.does: Selects all inbox items that have not been applied.
symbols.description: This function filters the item list to unapplied entries, extracts their IDs, and replaces the selected Set with those IDs. It drives the toolbar's select-all checkbox behavior.

symbols.does: Clears all selected inbox item IDs.
symbols.description: This function resets selection to an empty Set. It is used by both the select-all checkbox path and the explicit Clear action in the bulk toolbar.

symbols.does: Applies suggested categories for selected items that have suggestions.
symbols.description: This async function iterates selected IDs, finds each matching item, and applies its suggested category when present. It then clears selection and displays a toast using the number of selected IDs.

symbols.does: Runs autoTag sequentially for each selected item.
symbols.description: This function sets a bulk tagging flag, awaits autoTag for every selected ID, and clears the flag when complete. The flag disables the bulk retag button and changes its label while the operation is running.

symbols.does: Confirms and discards all selected inbox items.
symbols.description: This function asks for browser confirmation using the selected count, filters selected items out of the item list, and clears selection. It performs the bulk discard action from the toolbar.

symbols.does: Converts dropped or browsed files into inbox item records and starts tagging.
symbols.description: This async function loops through uploaded files, reads image files as data URLs, creates item metadata including ID and received time, prepends each item into state, and calls autoTag for the new ID. Non-image files are stored without dataUrl.

symbols.does: Classifies one inbox item into a category using window.claude.
symbols.description: This async function marks an item as thinking, retrieves it from state, and sends either image content plus filename or file metadata to Claude for classification. It normalizes the reply to a category ID, validates it against CATEGORIES, stores the suggested category, and falls back to imagery on invalid output or error.

symbols.does: Marks an item as applied to a category and emits applied feedback.
symbols.description: This function updates the matching item so appliedCat and suggestedCat both equal the chosen category. It also calls onApplied with the category and displays a toast naming the filed item and category label.

symbols.does: Removes one inbox item from the list.
symbols.description: This function filters the item with the given ID out of state. It is wired to the row discard button in InboxItem.

symbols.does: Applies every available suggestion on unapplied items.
symbols.description: This function iterates over items, and for each unapplied item with a suggested category, marks it applied and calls onApplied. It finishes by displaying a toast with the number of unapplied items that had suggestions.

symbols.does: Starts autoTag for every unapplied item that is not already thinking.
symbols.description: This function filters pending items to those not currently being classified and invokes autoTag for each one. It powers the Re-tag all control for the whole inbox list.

symbols.does: Removes all applied items from the inbox.
symbols.description: This function keeps only items without appliedCat in state and shows a toast indicating tagged items were cleared. It supports cleanup after filing decisions have been made.

symbols.does: Renders one inbox row with preview, metadata, tag state, category picker, and actions.
symbols.description: This component displays a checkbox, image thumbnail or file icon, filename, type, size, received time, visual-reading marker, tag status, and row-level controls. It lets the user apply the suggested tag, retag the item, discard it, or open a category button picker for manual application.
